summary.recommendedFuturePhase: URL Truth & SEO Governance remediation after audit approval

clusters.authorityRisk: Two backend-generated career family hash variants and the industry route describe the same semantic family in both locales.
clusters.canonicalOwnershipQuestion: Choose one backend canonical family slug and decide whether the industry page remains a differentiated taxonomy page or becomes a supporting page.
recommendedPlan: Freeze current public URLs until backend ownership is confirmed.
Identify backend source rows that produce the 37ec69bd and a0352070 variants.
Select one canonical family entity id and one canonical public family slug.
Prepare compatibility redirects only after sitemap, llms, canonical, and internal-link parity tests cover the migration.
Preserve the industry URL only if it has distinct industry taxonomy intent, title, schema identity, and internal links.
mustNotDoNow: Do not delete hashed URLs in frontend code.
Do not add frontend canonical overrides.
Do not redirect without backend canonical ownership and regression gates.

clusters.authorityRisk: Career family and career industry URLs share the same semantic slug across locales.
clusters.canonicalOwnershipQuestion: Confirm whether family hub and industry page are separate entity types with differentiated metadata/schema.
recommendedPlan: Keep both route families stable while auditing rendered titles, descriptions, JSON-LD identity, and internal links.
If both remain indexable, differentiate family-hub intent from industry-taxonomy intent.
If one is only a compatibility surface, plan redirect and sitemap removal in a later scoped PR.
mustNotDoNow: Do not collapse family and industry routes in this PR.
Do not invent frontend SEO metadata to create differentiation.
Do not change current indexed URLs before migration gates exist.

clusters.authorityRisk: Career family and career industry URLs share the same semantic slug across locales.
clusters.canonicalOwnershipQuestion: Confirm whether family hub and industry page are separate entity types with differentiated metadata/schema.
recommendedPlan: Keep both route families stable while auditing rendered titles, descriptions, JSON-LD identity, and internal links.
If both remain indexable, differentiate family-hub intent from industry-taxonomy intent.
If one is only a compatibility surface, plan redirect and sitemap removal in a later scoped PR.
mustNotDoNow: Do not collapse family and industry routes in this PR.
Do not invent frontend SEO metadata to create differentiation.
Do not change current indexed URLs before migration gates exist.

futureRemediationSequence: Backend source-of-truth audit for career family ids, slugs, locale mappings, and canonical entity ownership.
Rendered metadata/schema sampling for affected family and industry URLs.
Decision record: keep differentiated routes or consolidate compatibility variants.
Regression gates for sitemap, llms, canonical, hreflang, JSON-LD, redirects, and internal links.
Scoped migration PR only after backend ownership and compatibility rules are approved.

mergeSafety.reason: Career entity ambiguity should be resolved or explicitly governed before expanding graph/link surfaces.
